attacking_style: Explosive accelerations in final 4km, particularly on technical descents or punchy climbs. Favors repeated attacks rather than single long-range moves, often countering immediately after being caught.
team_tactics: Burns domestiques early through aggressive tempo control, leaving him isolated in finals. Unconventional tactics include entering breakaways while holding GC jersey and leading out teammates despite protected status.
terrain_preference: Cobbled classics (elite-level immediately), technical descents with high-speed gear (62-tooth), uphill sprints on 4-6% gradients. Surprisingly vulnerable on Tour de France cobbled stages despite Roubaix palmares.
vulnerability: Mechanicals at critical moments prove devastating when isolated. Strategic paralysis when domestiques depleted—defaults to reactive marking instead of initiating. Sprint finishing power inconsistent when following wheels.
key_patterns: Attacks inside final 4km on technical terrain, often descents or punchy climbs (3 instances)
Isolated early after team burns out, forcing reactive rather than proactive racing (3 instances)
Immediate world-class performance on specialized terrain without prior experience (cobbles, uphill sprints)
Tour de France form decline pattern: dominant early stages followed by weakness in week 2-3 (2022, 2023)
Mechanical issues disproportionately costly due to lack of chase support from depleted team (2 critical instances)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 shows declining pattern from Paris-Roubaix dominance (April) to caught breakaway at Tour (July). 2022 Tour cobbles underperformance echoes vulnerability when form not peak.
built_from: 2023 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
